**What it is:** Daily 10–15 minute lesson opener for AP English Language & Composition teachers. Two auto-generated versions (student + teacher) delivered M–F at 6am local. One-time 4-minute setup form. Zero manual work per teacher after setup.

**Target customer:** ~10,000 U.S. high school AP Lang teachers. Spends 8–15 hrs/week on article prep. Already pays out of pocket for EdTech tools. Active on Facebook AP Lang groups and AP Lang subreddit.

**The daily artifact:**
- Student version: 350–450 word nonfiction article (current within 7 days), 2 discussion questions, 2–3 AP-style MC (no answers), 1 writing prompt (Q1/Q2/Q3, rotates per D-001)
- Teacher version: all above + rhetorical devices pre-marked with para/line refs, MC answer key, sample strong responses, common misconceptions, AP exam alignment notes
- Delivery: email (default) + web link + Google Doc + PDF

**Pricing:** $19.99/mo solo, $190/yr annual, $14.99/seat/mo dept (5+ seats annual), "Contact us" for school/district. 14-day free trial, no card required. One plan, no Pro tier.

**Key decisions locked:**
- AP Lang only in 2026 (not AP Lit, not other subjects)
- Dual-version output by default, no toggle
- 350–450 word article target
- 2 discussion questions per brief
- Writing prompt framed as homework/extended writing, not part of opener
- Annual billing default + summer-as-feature (no pause option)
- No Pro tier in year 1
- D-001 (May 2, 2026): AP unit handling = mixed-by-default rotation (Q1/Q2/Q3), teacher-version footer shows current state with one-click lock override. "Current AP unit" setup field now optional with "skip if cycling" framing.
- D-004 (May 22, 2026): AP Lang canon (famous/commencement speeches: JFK, MLK, Wallace's Kenyon, Saunders, FDR, etc.) stays OUT of the default content cadence. Reaffirms D-002 item (3): September pilot ships current nonfiction, open/CC-commercial/public-domain, 350-450 words. Reasoning: teachers have no prep pain with the canon (already own it, public domain, curriculum guides spoon-feed it); mixing it in dilutes the "current within 7 days" value prop and pulls toward a different product. Canon is a post-MVP expansion lever ("Speech of the Week" Friday slot, or a classic thematically paired with the week's article) on the same delivery rails, and/or a discovery-interview probe ("When you teach rhetorical analysis with historical speeches, what's the friction there?") to confirm demand before building.
- D-002 (May 13, 2026): Build-readiness session. Locked 7 decisions: (1) Samuel is the quality bar, AP Lang reviewer only on a defined trigger; (2) hallucination control = sample-based audit (position 2); (3) article sourcing = open/CC-commercial/public-domain only (option D); (4) format-drift = hybrid now, regression suite at ~200 teachers; (5) editorial neutrality = 3-tier framework draft; (6) ops = Claude support agent post-MVP + optimized infra targeting ~95% margin; (7) timeline reframe (see below). Discovery script revised → Product Brief v1.1. Core throughline: build risk is a 2027 scale-phase problem; September 2026 is a sales/evidence problem solvable with zero code.

**Timeline (revised by D-002, May 13 — calendar-collision fix):**

- Task #2: Teacher-facing one-pager (paused, see below)

- Mid/late Aug → September: Manual MVP runs for real (4 weeks, hand-written briefs in live classrooms)

- October: convert pilots to paying
- ⚠️ Samuel's homework: verify the Wildfire fall-2026 application deadline. If spring 2027, calendar collision evaporates and timeline relaxes.

**Why:** Samuel wants The Garage evidence stack by September 2026. The Caught Up AI vehicle satisfies three criteria: concrete measurable pain, workflow-based moat, solo-buildable MVP phase.

**Update 2026-06-04 - Product design session; CONTENT ENGINE is the open blocker:**
Began designing the product (end-to-end journey map; spine agreed; paused before deep-designing the Landing stage). The session went deep on where the daily article comes from, which gates everything.
- D-005: Link / point-to-source model REJECTED. The teacher version marks up rhetorical devices INSIDE the text, so the article must be reproduced AND modified in the email; cannot be done on a remote link.
- D-006 (finding, verified by deep research): To reproduce-in-full, annotate inline, and sell, only PUBLIC DOMAIN / CC0 / CC-BY licenses qualify. CC-BY-SA's ShareAlike makes the paid teacher version freely redistributable, so treat as off. ALL NonCommercial (NC) out (paid product); ALL NoDerivatives (ND) out (annotation = derivative). The Conversation = CC-BY-ND (out); ProPublica = CC-BY-NC-ND (out).
- D-007 (finding, verified by spot-check): The free CC-BY/PD pool of CURRENT, argument-driven, classroom-safe nonfiction is too thin for a daily cadence. Global Voices (CC BY 3.0) is the lone genuine current source (~1-2 usable/wk, activism-skewed). The 7-day vs 30-day window is irrelevant; the constraint is licensing structure, not recency.
- D-008: Canon-as-backbone REJECTED (reaffirms D-004). No teacher prep pain, no moat, kills the subscription logic, and the modern canon is still copyrighted. Canon stays a supplement only.
- AI-quality scope (honest): AI can write rhetorically rich, hard-to-detect, useful ANALYSIS TEXT, but only with a human editor per piece; it cannot fully replicate authentic pathos / lived experience or supply a real rhetorical situation. So AI-at-quality = AI + human editor (labor), plus trust/disclosure tradeoffs. Fully automated = spottable/hollow.
- OPEN DECISION (resume here): the content engine. Cannot keep real + not-commissioned + current + daily + cheap + annotatable (empty set); must relax one. Honest finalists: LICENSE real op-eds (spend money; annotation/derivative rights are hard to get) vs AI-WITH-EDITING (spend labor; authenticity/trust tradeoffs). Other relaxations: drop to a 2-3x/week free product, or commission writers.
- Reframe to keep: sourcing difficulty IS the moat; the apparatus is the real IP, worth most on fresh content nobody else has covered.
- NEXT STEP proposed: draft a blind teacher test (unlabeled AI brief vs real brief) for discovery to settle whether real authorship is load-bearing. Alternative: resume the journey map at Landing.
- Does NOT block the fall manual MVP; this is the weakest link, tested by the MVP/discovery, not a stopper.
- Also covered (reusable): build-and-cost path sketch (Phase 0 manual ~$0-20/mo; Phase 1 semi-auto Base44 + Resend + Stripe ~$50-150/mo; Phase 2 automated ~$150-400/mo run + ~$3-25k one-time build); ~95% margin realistic; content cost flat in user count; biggest underestimated build risk = email deliverability.
- Full detail: vault 20_Conversations/2026-06-04 - Product design and content sourcing.md; license filter: 30_Knowledge/Caught Up AI - content licensing filter.md; project state: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Content-Sourcing.md.

**Update 2026-06-04 (cont.) - Blind test, AI side built:**
- Built the source-article Writing Manual (10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Writing-Manual.md), reverse-engineered from the verified released AP Lang exam Q2 corpus (2005-2024): open/build/close spine, single structural engine, long-vs-short AND syntactic-variety rhythm rules, earned-device palette, AI-tell blocklist (no em-dashes, banned diction/constructions), markability test, paste-ready generation brief. This is the editorial standard / apparatus IP for the content engine.
- Decision: for the blind teacher test, Claude writes the AI side only; Samuel supplies currently-published real op-eds to pair, strips labels, randomizes order. Topic lane = safe + pointed (evergreen, non-partisan).
- Wrote 3 AI essays to the manual (passion-as-career-advice myth; optimization/hustle culture; true-crime ethics), each in its own Google Doc on the Northwestern account (samuellevy2030@u.northwestern.edu). Backup: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Blind-Test-Essays.md.
- Next: Samuel reviews/owns the essays, selects+pairs the real comparators, builds the scoring instrument (guess AI vs real + which they would rather teach), runs in discovery to settle license-vs-AI-with-editing.
- Corpus correction to remember: Chavez nonviolence passage = 2015 Q2 rhetorical analysis, not a 2014 argument prompt.
- Detail: 20_Conversations/2026-06-04 - AP Lang writing manual and blind-test essays.md.

**Update 2026-06-04 (cont. 2) - Content-engine cost research; commissioning emerges as front-runner:**
- Ran cost research on the three relaxations (3 parallel research agents, sourced). LICENSING: price is minor, rights are fatal for major publishers (verbatim-only standard licenses; annotation = derivative = bespoke enterprise/legal deals + moral-rights gate; not solo-obtainable). Newsela is the only commercial reproduce+modify precedent (pays 175+ publishers, venture-funded; not copyable). Only surviving license channel = direct from independent/Substack writers who own their rights (~$150-600/piece; ~$1,650/mo at 2-3x/wk). FREE POOL: reduced cadence does NOT fix supply; only Global Voices (CC BY, ~1-3/wk) plus US-federal public-domain texts (rhetorically great, current, but partisan = neutrality risk). Honest free cadence: 1x/wk comfortable, 2x/wk strained, 3x/wk breaks.
- COMMISSION ORIGINALS (work-for-hire) promoted to front-runner: solves real authorship + full ownership/annotation + currency + classroom neutrality at once; ~$100/piece mid (400-600 wds), ~$860/mo (2x/wk) to ~$1,300/mo (3x/wk), flat in user count, break-even ~65 subs. Proof model: The Juice (original journalist/educator content, leveled, 6am, no verbatim reposting). Reduced cadence's real value is making commissioning affordable pre-revenue, not unlocking the free pool.
- Reframed finalists: commission originals (2-3x/wk) + free/PD/canon supplement on off-days is the front-runner; AI-with-editing is now the cost-saver variant of commissioning (today's formula critique weakens pure-AI); licensing survives only as direct-from-writers, which commissioning dominates. Blind-test question shifts to "does authentic authorship move teachers at all?"
- Feedback logged: rule-driven AI prose reads formulaic across pieces (feedback_ai_writing_formulaic.md).
- OPEN next step: design the commissioning pipeline (writer sourcing, work-for-hire contract, per-piece brief from the Writing Manual, budget) vs run the blind test first. Detail: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Content-Sourcing.md (2026-06-04 cost research section).

**Update 2026-06-04 (cont. 3) - commissioning OUT; "leave the article untouched" idea (candidate D-009):**
- Samuel ruled out commissioning writers.
- New idea: reproduce the article verbatim (untouched) and put rhetorical-device locations in a SEPARATE apparatus in the teacher brief, instead of inline markup. So the product needs only REPRODUCTION rights, not DERIVATIVE rights, dissolving the core blocker (D-006). Licensing moves from structurally impossible to a price/terms question.
- Preliminary read: unlocks mainstream reprint/syndication licenses (verbatim "as written" now fits, so AP/Reuters/Tribune/reprints are a cost question not a rights wall) and reopens CC-BY-ND sources (verbatim commercial repro allowed; article + separate apparatus = collection, not derivative); prize = The Conversation if its terms allow paid use. Does NOT fix NonCommercial (still out) or make reproduction free. Small pedagogy tradeoff (device map beside clean article).
- To verify: the derivative-vs-collection line (actual terms + a lawyer; keep article/apparatus physically separate); ND contract overlays (The Conversation is the swing factor); reprint pricing at cadence. Samuel asked whether to run this verification.
- Detail: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Content-Sourcing.md (2026-06-04 "later" section).

**Update 2026-06-04 (cont. 4) - D-009 VERIFIED (31-agent workflow): sound with conditions, free-daily dream is dead:**
- Legal model = SOUND WITH CONDITIONS: verbatim article + physically separate, non-interleaved apparatus = a collection not a derivative (17 USC 106(2)); well-reasoned but UNTESTED on these facts -> needs a written copyright-attorney opinion before launch (hard gate). Reproduction/distribution rights still engaged; fair use does not rescue full verbatim commercial copying.
- Opens new avenues but narrow/conditional: CC-BY-ND for verbatim paid reuse, standard reprint licenses become the right shape (but unaffordable), direct-from-writer licensing. THE CONVERSATION + KNOWABLE STILL CLOSED on a separate CONTRACT overlay (no selling separately / no systematic republishing) the design can't fix; The Conversation also mandates a tracking pixel (student-privacy). Newly-clean CC-BY-ND pool is thin: Singularity Hub (provisional) + OpenMind (conditional). Still closed: ProPublica (NC), NYT etc (terms), SA.
- Pricing: free-in-cash = federal PD (only confirmed base) + Global Voices (contact-gated, Left) + provisionals; real cost = curation labor ~5-10 hrs/wk. Paid all poor for small budget (AP ~$1,600/mo smallest, Tribune high-hundreds-thousands, NYT/WaPo/WSJ ~$9-20k/yr daily, Reuters enterprise, CCC rail-only). Direct-from-writer only one that scales to 1/wk; price TBD by blind test. Unit econ: ~$14 net/sub, ~16-46 subs fund one writer-piece/wk.
- 3-5/wk? NOT on free alone: honest floor ~2/wk, 3 typical, 4-5 rare; four-filter squeeze; only federal PD confirmed today. Free + ~1 paid writer/wk -> steadier 3-4. Reliable 5-day not deliverable free. Don't promise cadence until a 2-4 wk real pull counts survivors.
- Recommended engine: federal PD base + gated free (Global Voices, Singularity Hub) + OpenMind upside + ~1 direct-from-writer piece/wk later. Format discipline load-bearing (bit-for-bit article, zero inline labels, separate apparatus, short-phrase quotes by location).
- OPEN next steps (ordered): (1) volume pressure-test FIRST; (2) clear 3 free gates; (3) blind-test writer channel for prices; (4) scope state/local + institutional CC0; (5) apparatus template; (6) lawyer opinion = launch gate. Samuel to pick which to start. Full memo: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Licensing-Verification.md.

**Update 2026-06-05 - Pivot toward full AI-generation; v2 prose-engine research prompt built:**
- Paid-writer / commissioning path treated as off (cost + the earlier ruling). Samuel leaning to FULL AI-GENERATION: Claude writes each piece from scratch off verified facts (facts are not copyrightable), NOT reproducing/paraphrasing source prose. This sidesteps the licensing wall (D-006/D-009 were all about reproducing others' text) and relocates the main risk to editorial: current-events accuracy + the AI-prose-reads-same-y problem (feedback_ai_writing_formulaic).
- Target clarified: emulate the PROFESSIONAL SOURCE passages students analyze on the AP Lang exam (Q2 rhetorical-analysis + Q1 synthesis sources), NOT student essays. Honest reframe: no single "AP Lang style"; the exam spans centuries/genres, so the target is a PALETTE of registers and success = blind-read indistinguishability across that palette. Emulate craft, not topics. Accuracy stays a separate hard requirement.
- Built a paste-ready deep-research COMMISSION via a 10-agent workflow (ground exam facts + real corpus -> draft through 4 lenses: rhetoric scholar / stylometry / AI-tells / variety-system -> adversarial critique -> synthesize). Saved at 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/AP-Lang-Research-Prompt.md (em-dashes stripped, ASCII-normalized).
- When run, it produces v2 of the Writing Manual: Style-Palette, Structural-Templates, Register-Specs (qualitative + stylometric bands), Anti-Tell-List, Cross-Piece-Sameness-Rubric, Annotated-Exemplars, Generation-Briefs, Blind-Test-Protocol, and a master Prose-Engine-Research-Report. Supersedes (does not overwrite) Writing-Manual.md v1, whose known weakness is a single template/rhythm rule that would itself become a detectable house style.
- OPEN next: Samuel skims 6 knobs (register count, corpus span, blind-test scale, piece length, detector reliance, exemplar topics), adjusts, then runs the commission from the vault folder. Conversation: 20_Conversations/2026-06-05 - AP Lang prose engine research prompt.md.

**Update 2026-06-05 (build) - v2 prose engine DELIVERED:**
- Ran the commission as a 56-agent ultracode workflow (~57 min, 3.2M subagent tokens). All 10 deliverables written to 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/: Style-Palette, Structural-Templates, Register-Specs, Anti-Tell-List, Cross-Piece-Sameness-Rubric, Accuracy-Guardrail, Annotated-Exemplars, Blind-Test-Protocol, Generation-Briefs, Prose-Engine-Research-Report. Writing-Manual.md v1 left intact with a supersession note at top.
- PALETTE (7 registers): The Ledger (impersonal evidence-forward anchor/default), The Open Letter (deferential periodic appeal to a named power), The Reckoning (civic indictment, charge to act), The Tribute (epideictic consecration, benediction close), The Long Think (essayistic reasoning to an earned turn; annexes controlled satire), The Witness Stand (first-person memoir-as-evidence), The Long Look (descriptive awe, withholds moral judgment). Distinctness audit hard-gated the two near-merge pairs (Reckoning/Tribute via close-move; Long Think/Long Look via commit-vs-render, Louv reassigned to Long Look).
- Corpus: 49 entries, provenance re-audited (zero false exam-confirmations found; all 3 v1 corrections held; minor date/slot refinements only). Stylometric bands independently recomputed for 4 public-domain registers and held (no band contradicted). One material claim correction logged (arXiv 2506.21817 mis-cited for connective overuse).
- PILOT IS A PRE-SCREEN ONLY, not a pass signal. Automated proxy went 7/7 identifying the machine piece, but that is CONFOUNDED: the exemplars still carry [PLACEHOLDER] tags (a dead giveaway) and topic/era differs from the genuine comparison passages. Band check: only 2/8 pieces fully in band (6 drift on rhythm/openers/length pole). Both are documented honestly in Blind-Test-Protocol.md sec 9. The decisive test is the HUMAN AP-Lang-teacher panel (protocol fully specified, Samuel to run), which requires placeholder-free (verified-fact) pieces and topic-matched pairs.
- Post-build fixes by Claude: removed one banned word ("leverage" noun -> "the upper hand") from the Witness Stand exemplar; confirmed all 10 files em-dash/emoji clean; added the supersession note to Writing-Manual.md.
- OPEN next (Samuel's call): (1) read Style-Palette + Register-Specs + Generation-Briefs + Annotated-Exemplars; (2) calibrate the 6 out-of-band exemplars back into their bands and feed fixes into Register-Specs/Anti-Tell-List (the iteration loop); (3) to run the real blind test, produce placeholder-free pieces with verified facts and recruit AP-Lang teachers (ties to existing teacher list/outreach tracker). Conversation: 20_Conversations/2026-06-05 - Prose engine v2 build.md.

**Update 2026-06-05 (Proxy Run 2 - cleaner proxy):**
- Ran a cleaner self-proxy (72-agent workflow): 14 placeholder-free pieces from the REAL Generation-Briefs (2/register), each paired with a MODERN register-matched genuine human passage, 3 blind AI judges/pair. Result: 36/36 (100%) the AI judge still identified the machine piece, SAME as Run 1 even with the placeholder + era confounds removed. So the leak is in the WRITING, not test hygiene. (Long Think not blind-read: its genuine-passage fetch failed; 12 pairs x 3 = 36.) Band check: only 2/14 in band; opener variety is the #1 miss (too subject-first across nearly every register). Full file: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Proxy-Pilot-Run-2.md.
- CONSENSUS TELLS (the real payoff, what the judges named): (1) manufactured symmetry (not-X-but-Y, stacked tricolons, fixed-beat anaphora); (2) tidy closing buttons / aphoristic kickers on nearly every paragraph (esp. negation-correction closers); (3) frictionlessness (no awkwardness, no wasted/off-thesis detail, uniform polish = the tell); (4) too-tidy invented specifics (round numbers, placeholder-y proper nouns); (5) over-engineered keyword loops / ring composition; (6) reader-directing signposting + labeled enumeration. Human-contrast textures the pieces LACK: disfluency, run-ons/comma splices, spoken asides, idiosyncratic coinages, load-bearing verifiable detail, tonal unevenness, taking a side or trailing off.
- KEY STRATEGIC READ: (a) the spec's own emphasis on LEGIBLE rhetorical architecture is partly manufacturing the symmetry that reads as machine, the fix is a deliberate FRICTION / ANTI-SYMMETRY layer, not more rules; (b) a frontier AI judge in forced-choice is likely near-saturated (catches almost everything) and is partly cued by complete-tidy-WRITTEN-piece vs spoken/excerpt genuine comparisons, so 100% AI-judge does NOT equal 100% human; (c) do NOT keep optimizing against the AI judge (overfitting risk, locked param P5) -- the AI-proxy has now delivered its value (the cue list). The product-relevant bar is indistinguishable-to-HUMANS, not beating a detector.
- NEXT: build the friction/anti-symmetry layer into Register-Specs + Anti-Tell-List from these six cues, fix opener variety and the per-register punctuation/sentence-type band misses, regenerate, then run the DECISIVE human AP-Lang teacher panel per Blind-Test-Protocol.md (the only instrument that can return near-chance). Conversation: 20_Conversations/2026-06-05 - Prose engine v2 build.md.

**Update 2026-06-05 (v2.1 friction layer + sample essays):**
- DONE (first half of the NEXT above): integrated the six Run-2 consensus tells into Anti-Tell-List.md as a read-first "Friction / Anti-Symmetry Layer" (v2.1) plus mechanical-pass steps 9-11. The six fixes: cap manufactured symmetry (limit not-X-but-Y and autopilot tricolons), kill the tidy closing-button reflex (let paragraphs end flat), require deliberate friction (one inert/off-thesis detail + one uneven turn per piece), make invented specifics un-tidy (no round numbers / placeholder-y names; in production these are real verified facts), drop over-engineered keyword loops / ring composition, cut reader-directing signposting and labeled enumeration. Note: did NOT yet push these into Register-Specs per-register, and did NOT regenerate the 7 exemplars in Annotated-Exemplars (still the v2 versions); those remain open.
- Produced 3 sample essays from the v2.1 manual (The Ledger = state right-to-repair law; The Reckoning = transit fare-hike accountability; The Witness Stand = first-gen student + financial-aid verification) in a Google Doc titled "Caught Up AI - Friction-Layer Sample Essays (2026-06-05)" for Samuel to review. Facts illustrative, not fact-checked.
- Caveat: the friction layer is directional (derived from the AI-judge proxy Run 2, not humans). The decisive test remains the human AP-Lang panel.
